Lincang.name: Lincang
Lincang.about: Lincang is a coal-hosted germanium deposit in Yunnan Province, southwestern China. The germanium concentration in the local coal beds is around 850 parts per million, the highest commercially mined anywhere in the world. The metal accumulated when hydrothermal fluids from the underlying Lincang granite batholith infiltrated ancient swamp coal seams over geological time, leaching germanium from the granite into organic-rich sediments that later compacted into coal. Reserves are estimated at over 1,000 tonnes of contained germanium. Operated by Yunnan Lincang Xinyuan Germanium Industry.
Lincang.risk: State-affiliated operator under export licensing regime; zero direct western access.
risks: State-affiliated operator under export licensing regime; zero direct western access.
Chinese export controls limit westward flow
Single operator concentration risk
Lincang.inv: Highest-grade germanium deposit in the world. Sets the upstream cost floor for Chinese primary production — anyone producing outside China must contend with Lincang's grade advantage when competing on price.

Wulantuga.name: Wulantuga
Wulantuga.about: Wulantuga is a germanium-bearing lignite deposit in Xilingol League, Inner Mongolia, China. By contained germanium reserves it is the largest deposit in the world at approximately 1,600 tonnes, but the average grade of around 137 ppm is significantly lower than Lincang and the lignite ash chemistry makes commercial extraction technically difficult. Only an estimated 15-25% of the theoretical germanium content is recovered in practice. Operated by Shengli Coal Group as part of routine thermal coal mining for Chinese power generators.
Wulantuga.risk: State ownership means output is policy-driven rather than price-responsive.
risks: State ownership means output is policy-driven rather than price-responsive.
Low recovery rate limits supply upside
No western investment access
Wulantuga.inv: Largest known germanium deposit by reserve size but only ~15-25% of theoretical content is commercially recovered. Theoretical supply headroom exists but isn't being realized by current operators.

Yimin.name: Yimin
Yimin.about: Yimin is a germanium-bearing lignite basin in Inner Mongolia, China, containing an estimated 4,000 tonnes of total germanium resources distributed across multiple smaller deposits. Total resource size is larger than Wulantuga, but grade is lower and the deposits are more geographically dispersed across the basin. Recovery rates are correspondingly lower, and output is fragmented across multiple smaller state-affiliated coal operators rather than concentrated under a single major. As a result, the basin's contribution to actual refined germanium output is much smaller than its raw resource size suggests.
Yimin.risk: Fragmented operator base, opaque production data.
risks: Fragmented operator base, opaque production data.
Grade too low at current prices
State control limits western access
Yimin.inv: Additional Chinese coal-hosted germanium resource. Worked by various smaller state-affiliated operators; low recovery rates, limited price responsiveness.

Huize.name: Huize
Huize.about: Huize is a zinc-lead ore deposit in Yunnan Province, China, with significant germanium content concentrated in the sphalerite zinc mineralization. The germanium content is recovered as a byproduct of zinc smelting rather than through standalone germanium mining — a different extraction pathway from the coal-hosted deposits at Lincang or Wulantuga. The zinc smelter byproduct route is technically simpler and more economically efficient than coal-ash extraction, which is part of why Huize anchors the largest single primary germanium output stream globally. Operated by Yunnan Chihong with reserves estimated at 443 tonnes of contained germanium.
Huize.risk: Output tied to zinc pricing, not germanium demand signals.
risks: Output tied to zinc pricing, not germanium demand signals.
Ge output tied to zinc production decisions
Chinese listed - limited western access
Huize.inv: Primary source of germanium recovered through Yunnan Chihong's zinc smelter operations. The zinc-ore pathway complements the coal-hosted pathway as Chinese germanium's second extraction route.

Yiliang + SYGT.name: Yiliang + SYGT
Yiliang + SYGT.about: Yiliang and SYGT are additional zinc-lead deposits in Yunnan Province with germanium content, located in the same regional Huize-Yiliang carbonate-hosted zinc-lead district. Combined proven germanium reserves are around 186 tonnes — smaller than Huize but feeding into the same Yunnan Chihong-operated smelter system. The deposits are part of the broader resource base that anchors Chihong's vertically integrated zinc-to-germanium-to-GeCl₄ pathway, supplementing Huize as concentrate feedstock for the Qujing smelter.
Yiliang + SYGT.risk: Limited public disclosure on reserves and production.
risks: Limited public disclosure on reserves and production.
Regional concentration risk
Ownership fragmentation
Yiliang + SYGT.inv: Additional Yunnan zinc-lead resources with germanium content. Feeds into Chinese primary germanium supply via associated smelter operations.

Lincang Xinyuan.name: Lincang Xinyuan
Lincang Xinyuan.about: Yunnan Lincang Xinyuan Germanium Industry is a Chinese specialty mining and refining company listed on the Shenzhen Stock Exchange and headquartered in Kunming, Yunnan Province. It mines coal-hosted germanium at the Lincang deposit — the world's highest-grade germanium-bearing coal beds at around 850 ppm — and refines the recovered material into germanium dioxide, zone-refined ingots, photovoltaic-grade GeCl₄, infrared optics components, and gallium arsenide wafers. The company is one of the few publicly listed pure-play germanium producers globally, and its product range spans across most major germanium end uses.
Lincang Xinyuan.risk: Chinese export controls limit westward flow of output.
risks: Chinese export controls limit westward flow of output.
A-share listing limits western investors
Single-site concentration risk
Lincang Xinyuan.inv: Shenzhen-listed operator of the highest-grade Chinese germanium deposit. Provides indirect public investor exposure to Chinese germanium supply, though A-share access is limited for non-Chinese investors.

Shengli Coal Group.name: Shengli Coal Group
Shengli Coal Group.about: Shengli Coal Group is a Chinese state-owned coal mining group operating in Inner Mongolia, primarily working the Wulantuga lignite basin. The group's main business is thermal coal supply to Chinese power generators, with germanium recovered as a byproduct from the coal fly ash and combustion residues. Wulantuga is the largest known germanium-bearing coal deposit by reserve size, though the low concentration and complex extraction process mean only 15-25% of theoretical germanium content is commercially recovered. Shengli is not publicly listed and operates under regional state-owned enterprise governance.
Shengli Coal Group.risk: Opaque production data and no western investment vehicle.
risks: Opaque production data and no western investment vehicle.
Power generation is primary — Ge always secondary
Policy shifts directly impact output
Shengli Coal Group.inv: State-owned operator of the largest reserve-size germanium deposit. Output is policy-driven rather than price-responsive; limits upside even during supply constraints.

Yunnan Chihong.name: Yunnan Chihong
Yunnan Chihong.about: Yunnan Chihong Zinc & Germanium is a Chinese state-affiliated mining and smelting company listed on the Shanghai Stock Exchange and headquartered in Qujing, Yunnan Province. It mines zinc, lead, and germanium-bearing ore at the Huize and Yiliang deposits in Yunnan, smelts the concentrates at integrated facilities in Qujing, and produces zinc, lead, sulfuric acid, silver, and germanium products including high-purity germanium tetrachloride and zone-melted ingots. Chihong is the largest single primary germanium producer globally and one of four operators in China with fiber-grade GeCl₄ capacity. The company traces back to 1951.
Yunnan Chihong.risk: Chinese export controls limit direct western access to the output.
risks: Chinese export controls limit direct western access to the output.
Ge output tied to zinc production
Zinc price weakness reduces Ge output
Yunnan Chihong.inv: Largest single source of primary germanium globally. Shanghai-listed — primary A-share vehicle for Chinese germanium exposure. Integrated zinc-ore-to-refined-germanium pathway through the Huize smelter.

Lincang Xinyuan Refinery.name: Lincang Xinyuan Refinery
Lincang Xinyuan Refinery.about: Lincang Xinyuan Refinery is the downstream refining division of Yunnan Lincang Xinyuan Germanium Industry, the Shenzhen-listed Chinese pure-play germanium operator. The refinery converts crude germanium recovered from the Lincang coal deposit into a full product range — germanium dioxide, zone-refined ingots, photovoltaic-grade GeCl₄, infrared germanium components, and gallium arsenide wafers — for Chinese semiconductor, fiber, and photovoltaic customers. As an integrated downstream of the same parent company, the refinery's output volumes track parent group disclosure rather than a separate corporate identity.
stats: ~40 t/yr Ge refined (linked to parent SZSE:002428)
Lincang Xinyuan Refinery.risk: US export ban directly impacts western revenue.
risks: US export ban cuts wafer sales to western satellite manufacturers
Single-facility risk
Trade policy exposure
Lincang Xinyuan Refinery.inv: Part of Lincang Xinyuan (002428.SZ). Wafer business commands significant premiums over commodity Ge.

Yunnan Chihong Refinery.name: Yunnan Chihong Refinery
Yunnan Chihong Refinery.about: Yunnan Chihong Refinery is the downstream refining and chemical conversion division of Yunnan Chihong Zinc & Germanium, the Shanghai-listed Chinese state-affiliated zinc-lead miner and smelter. The refinery takes crude germanium recovered from Huize zinc concentrate smelting and converts it into refined germanium products — germanium dioxide, zone-melted ingots, fiber-grade germanium tetrachloride, and zinc-lead refined products. As one of four operators in China with fiber-grade GeCl₄ capacity, it is a key supplier to Chinese fiber preform manufacturers. Output is reported within Chihong's parent group disclosures.
stats: ~66 t/yr Ge refined (linked to parent SHSE:600497)
Yunnan Chihong Refinery.risk: Output primarily stays within Chinese domestic ecosystem.
risks: Output primarily stays within Chinese domestic ecosystem.
Single-facility concentration
Dependent on zinc smelter economics
Yunnan Chihong Refinery.inv: One of four fiber-grade GeCl₄ facilities in China. Integrated with upstream Chihong zinc-ore germanium extraction. Major feeder of Chinese domestic fiber preform production at YOFC, Hengtong, and ZTT.

Chinese State GeCl4 Plants.name: Chinese State GeCl₄ Plants
Chinese State GeCl4 Plants.about: Multiple Chinese state-linked processors produce GeCl₄ at various scales and purity levels. Includes Nanjing Germanium, GRINM Goujing (formerly Vital Materials), and Yunnan Lincang Xinyuan Germanium Industry. Collectively serve China’s domestic fiber optic market alongside Yunnan Chihong.
Chinese State GeCl4 Plants.risk: Export controls block western access. MOFCOM licensed for any international shipments.
risks: Export controls block all western supply
Aggregated opacity — actual output uncertain
State directive risk could redirect supply to strategic uses
Chinese State GeCl4 Plants.inv: No direct western investment vehicle. Exposure only via Chinese state-owned enterprise listings.

Corning.name: Corning
Corning.about: Invented low-loss optical fiber in 1970. World’s largest western fiber manufacturer operating across Optical Communications, Display, Specialty Materials, Environmental, and Life Sciences. Optical communications is the growth engine — Q3 2025 revenue hit $1.65B (+33% YoY) with enterprise sales surging 58% on AI network demand.
Corning.risk: GeO₂/GeCl₄ supply constraint limits capacity expansion.
risks: GeO₂/GeCl₄ supply constraint limits capacity
Significant China revenue exposure (~$1B+ annually)
Overcapacity risk if datacenter buildout slows
Corning.inv: GLW is the most liquid western proxy for AI infrastructure fiber demand. Multi-year datacenter backlogs give rare earnings visibility.

Prysmian.name: Prysmian
Prysmian.about: World’s largest cable company — energy cables, telecom cables, and submarine cable systems. 15% global fiber market share, the single largest. Acquired Encore Wire to expand North American footprint. Covers full cable value chain from preform manufacturing through finished cable.
Prysmian.risk: European telecom capex cycles. 18–24 month preform expansion lag.
risks: More exposed to European telecom capex cycles
Italian listing reduces liquidity for US investors
Currency risk (EUR-denominated)
Prysmian.inv: PRY offers terrestrial and subsea exposure. Subsea demand is driven by AI hyperscalers building private subsea networks.

Fujikura.name: Fujikura
Fujikura.about: Developed the world’s first optical fiber in 1959. Revenue from Information Technology (fiber, cables), Electronics (PCBs), Automotive (wiring harnesses), and Energy (power cables). Over 50% of revenue from the US. ~75% of optical fiber output exported. Stock surged 160% in 2025 on AI data center demand.
Fujikura.risk: Full Umicore dependency for GeCl₄ — no domestic Japanese alternative.
risks: Full Umicore dependency — no domestic Japanese GeCl₄ production
Yen depreciation impacts USD returns
Post-AI-surge valuation re-rating risk if growth decelerates
Fujikura.inv: TSE-listed with meaningful western investor access. Closest pure-play on the AI fiber demand + germanium supply constraint thesis.

Sumitomo Electric.name: Sumitomo Electric
Sumitomo Electric.about: One of Japan’s largest diversified industrial companies. Business includes Automotive (wiring harnesses — largest segment), Infocommunications (optical fiber, cables, photonic devices), Electronics, Environment & Energy, and Industrial Materials. Optical fiber is a meaningful but minority portion of total revenue.
Sumitomo Electric.risk: Fiber is a minority of revenue — diversified industrial reduces direct germanium exposure.
risks: Fiber is a minority of revenue — auto wiring harness dominates valuation
TSE listing limits western direct access
Auto wiring harness slowdown could weigh on overall valuation
Sumitomo Electric.inv: Unique dual exposure: AI fiber demand and EV wiring harness demand. Less pure-play than Fujikura given diversified revenue base.

Shin-Etsu.name: Shin-Etsu
Shin-Etsu.about: Japan’s largest chemical company. World leader in PVC, semiconductor silicon wafers, and silicones. Optical fiber preform is part of the Electronics Materials segment. Produces preforms at Kashima (Japan), plus two China JVs — Shin-Etsu Jiangsu (with Fasten Group) and Shin-Etsu YOFC (Hubei, with YOFC).
Shin-Etsu.risk: Silicon wafer oversupply risk if AI chip demand cycles down.
risks: Silicon wafer oversupply risk if AI chip demand cycles
Japanese listed — limited western direct access
Yen exposure for USD investors
Shin-Etsu.inv: Unique dual-exposure play: AI chip demand drives silicon wafer volumes AND AI datacenter buildout drives fiber preform volumes.

YOFC.name: YOFC
YOFC.about: World’s largest optical fiber preform manufacturer by capacity. China’s dominant fiber and cable producer. Vertically integrated from preform to cable. JVs with Shin-Etsu for advanced preform technology. International plants in Mexico, Indonesia, South Africa, Brazil, and Poland. Supplies China Mobile (30% of their fiber needs).
YOFC.risk: Chinese state-backed. Geopolitically complex. Corning JV creates IP exposure.
risks: Geopolitical risk — Chinese state ownership
Corning JV creates IP transfer concerns
Export control risk limits western sales potential
YOFC.inv: HKEX-listed with more western accessibility than SSE. The Corning JV structure creates a direct link between Chinese Ge supply and western fiber manufacturing IP.

Vertiv.name: Vertiv
Vertiv.about: Vertiv makes the power distribution, uninterruptible power supply, and thermal management systems that every datacenter depends on. AI GPU clusters consume 40-100kW per rack versus 5-10kW for traditional compute — Vertiv is the primary beneficiary of this density explosion.
stats: GPU clusters require 5-10x more cooling than CPUs
Vertiv.risk: AI density driving unprecedented thermal load per rack.
risks: Backlog-driven revenue creates lumpy delivery timing
High valuations leave limited margin of safety
Supply chain constraints on transformers and cooling components
Vertiv.inv: VRT is arguably the purest AI infrastructure play outside of Nvidia. Every GPU cluster needs Vertiv power and cooling systems. Revenue growing >20% annually driven entirely by AI datacenter buildout.

Equinix.name: Equinix
Equinix.about: Equinix provides colocation space, power, and physical interconnection between networks. The Equinix fabric connects 10,000+ networks globally — it is the physical internet exchange infrastructure that routes traffic between cloud providers, enterprises, and content networks.
Equinix.risk: AI workloads require owned capacity, not colo — structural headwind.
risks: Hyperscalers increasingly building their own infrastructure rather than using colo
AI training clusters prefer owned facilities for power density control
Interest rate sensitivity as a REIT with significant debt
Equinix.inv: EQIX is a REIT with stable recurring revenue. Less direct AI exposure than Vertiv — hyperscalers prefer to own their AI training facilities rather than colocate them. The interconnection business (charging for cross-connects between networks) is durable and high-margin.

CoreWeave.name: CoreWeave
CoreWeave.about: CoreWeave is an AI-native cloud built entirely around GPU compute. Unlike AWS or Azure which offer GPUs as a small fraction of their portfolio, CoreWeave's entire infrastructure is optimized for AI training and inference. Rents GPU capacity to AI companies and enterprises that cannot get enough capacity from hyperscalers.
CoreWeave.risk: Highly concentrated customer base. Microsoft is ~60% of revenue.
risks: ~60% revenue from Microsoft creates existential concentration risk
Massive debt load from GPU purchases (~$8B+)
Nvidia dependency — entire business model depends on Nvidia GPU supply
CoreWeave.inv: CRWV IPO'd in March 2025. The only pure-play GPU cloud stock. Extraordinary growth but extreme customer concentration risk with Microsoft as dominant customer.

Schneider Electric.name: Schneider Electric
Schneider Electric.about: Schneider Electric makes the switchgear, PDUs, DCIM software, and prefabricated datacenter modules that sit upstream of servers in every datacenter power chain. Also operates EcoStruxure — a datacenter management platform monitoring power and cooling efficiency.
Schneider Electric.risk: Less pure-play than Vertiv — diversified industrial conglomerate.
risks: Diversified revenue base dilutes AI datacenter growth contribution
European listing and currency risk for USD investors
Competition from Vertiv and Eaton in core power distribution market
Schneider Electric.inv: SU offers European-listed AI infrastructure exposure with a more diversified risk profile than Vertiv. Slower growth but more stable. The prefabricated datacenter module business is a strong AI tailwind.

AECOM.name: AECOM
AECOM.about: AECOM designs and manages construction of hyperscale datacenter campuses — the physical buildings, electrical infrastructure, cooling systems, and site development that house AI compute. Every major new hyperscale campus requires engineering firms like AECOM for permitting, design, and construction management.
AECOM.risk: Construction is cyclical. Revenue recognized on project completion.
risks: Construction revenue is lumpy and project-dependent
Labor and materials cost inflation squeezes margins
Less direct AI exposure than infrastructure equipment makers
AECOM.inv: ACM is an indirect AI play through datacenter construction boom. Multi-billion dollar hyperscale buildouts create sustained engineering demand. More stable than hardware plays but less direct AI exposure.

Microsoft.name: Microsoft
Microsoft.about: Microsoft is the largest single consumer of fiber optic cable and datacenter infrastructure globally. Its exclusive cloud relationship with OpenAI makes Azure the primary deployment platform for GPT-4 and successors. The Copilot integration across Office 365 (1.4B users) creates the largest AI inference workload in existence.
Microsoft.risk: Massive AI capex commitment. $80B+ in datacenter spend (2025).
risks: OpenAI relationship is non-exclusive at the model level
Azure losing market share to AWS in some enterprise segments
Regulatory scrutiny of OpenAI partnership in EU and UK
Microsoft.inv: MSFT is the most accessible AI infrastructure play at the hyperscale level. The OpenAI relationship creates durable AI revenue moats. Trading at ~32x forward earnings — premium but justified by AI growth trajectory.

Google.name: Google
Google.about: Google pioneered large-scale AI infrastructure. Its Tensor Processing Units (TPUs) are purpose-built AI accelerators that rival Nvidia GPUs at scale. DeepMind and Google Brain merged into Google DeepMind — the world's most published AI research organization. GCP is the third-largest cloud platform globally.
Google.risk: Search revenue under threat from AI-native search alternatives.
risks: Search revenue disruption from AI-native search (Perplexity, ChatGPT Search)
Antitrust scrutiny on search monopoly
GCP continues to lag AWS and Azure in enterprise market share
Google.inv: GOOGL offers AI exposure plus the most diversified revenue base of any hyperscaler — search, YouTube, GCP, hardware. The TPU custom silicon strategy reduces Nvidia dependency and improves margins at scale.

Amazon.name: Amazon
Amazon.about: AWS is the world's largest cloud platform and the primary infrastructure layer for thousands of AI startups. Amazon Bedrock provides API access to models from Anthropic, Meta, Mistral, and others. Trainium chips are purpose-built for AI training at AWS scale.
Amazon.risk: AWS AI revenue growing but Bedrock faces stiff competition.
risks: Bedrock's multi-model strategy may commoditize AI API revenue
Trainium chip adoption slower than hoped vs Nvidia GPUs
Retail segment margins remain thin despite logistics improvements
Amazon.inv: AMZN offers AI exposure plus the strongest e-commerce and logistics moats of any tech company. AWS is ~75% of Amazon operating income. The Anthropic investment (~$4B) provides strategic AI model access.

Meta.name: Meta
Meta.about: Meta operates approximately 30 major hyperscale datacenters and has committed to reaching 10GW of total datacenter power capacity by the end of 2026. Meta's $6 billion multi-year supply agreement with Corning — equal to Corning's entire 2025 optical-communications revenue — is the single largest fiber supply contract in history. Meta's Project Waterworth is the longest submarine cable ever built at 50,000km.
Meta.risk: Open-source AI strategy — Llama models reduce Meta's AI moat over time.
risks: Open-source AI strategy reduces Meta's AI differentiation moat
Social platform revenue base subject to advertising cycle
10GW build timeline requires flawless construction execution
Meta.inv: META (NASDAQ) is the most aggressive AI infrastructure builder among social platforms. Its $6B Corning agreement is the most tangible expression of hyperscaler fiber demand — locking in supply that smaller buyers cannot access. Meta's open-source AI strategy (Llama) may limit direct AI monetization but reduces dependency on OpenAI-licensed models.

xAI.name: xAI
xAI.about: xAI built the Colossus supercomputer in Memphis in 122 days — the fastest large-scale GPU cluster deployment in history. Grok 3 is competitive with GPT-4o and Claude 3.5 on benchmarks. Deep integration with X (Twitter) provides unique real-time data advantage for training.
xAI.risk: Private company. No direct investment vehicle currently.
risks: No direct investment access
Elon Musk concentration risk across Tesla, SpaceX, X, xAI simultaneously
Competing against Microsoft+OpenAI, Google, Amazon with deeper resources
xAI.inv: No direct investment vehicle. Monitor for IPO. Indirect exposure via Nvidia (GPU supplier) and Tesla (Elon relationship and shared compute resources). Series C at ~$50B valuation implies significant upside if Grok achieves meaningful enterprise adoption.

Oracle.name: Oracle
Oracle.about: Oracle is the fastest-growing major cloud platform driven entirely by AI infrastructure demand. xAI chose OCI for Colossus overflow capacity. The US government's $500B Stargate initiative includes Oracle as a primary infrastructure partner alongside Microsoft and SoftBank. Oracle's database dominance in enterprise creates a natural migration path to OCI.
Oracle.risk: Late cloud entrant. OCI growing fast but from small base.
risks: OCI starting from a much smaller base than AWS, Azure, GCP
Database licensing revenue declining as enterprises migrate to cloud
Stargate project timelines and funding are uncertain
Oracle.inv: ORCL is the most underappreciated AI infrastructure play among large-cap tech. OCI growing faster than any major cloud platform. Stargate partnership with US government creates durable, long-term contracted revenue.

Alcatel SN.name: Alcatel SN
Alcatel SN.about: Alcatel Submarine Networks is one of three companies controlling over 60% of global submarine cable wet-plant revenue. Recently transferred to French state ownership — underscoring governments' heightened interest in controlling critical subsea infrastructure. Partners with hyperscalers (Google, Meta, Microsoft, Amazon) building private submarine cable systems. Signed partnership with leading cloud provider for next-generation submarine cable system in July 2025.
Alcatel SN.risk: Recently transferred to French state ownership — geopolitical asset.
risks: Geopolitical risk around cable routes and landing rights
Subsea projects have 3-5 year lead times — lumpy revenue
State ownership may affect commercial flexibility
Alcatel SN.inv: Exposure via Nokia (NOKIA HE) prior to divestiture. Now French state asset. AI hyperscalers are funding private subsea cables directly, driving unprecedented demand.

SubCom.name: SubCom
SubCom.about: SubCom is the leading US submarine cable system supplier, combining cable manufacturing with marine installation vessels — a critical vertical integration that limits competition. SubCom controls a major share of US government-approved submarine cable projects given national security scrutiny of Chinese competitors. Expanding marine fulfillment capabilities to meet surging demand from 40 new submarine cable systems entering service in 2026.
risks: Private — no investment access
Revenue concentration in large contracts
US government scrutiny of subsea cable routes
SubCom.inv: No direct investment — private Cerberus portfolio company. An IPO or strategic sale would be significant given AI-driven subsea demand.

NEC.name: NEC
NEC.about: NEC Corporation has deployed over 400,000 km of undersea cables worldwide, making it one of the most experienced submarine cable installers globally. Dominant in the Asia-Pacific submarine cable market. Unveiled new submarine cable system for Asia in September 2025 to meet growing data traffic. Competes with SubCom and Alcatel Submarine Networks for hyperscaler private cable projects.
stats: New system for Asian AI traffic
NEC.risk: Subsea is a minority of NEC's total revenue.
risks: Subsea is minority of revenue
TSE listing limits western access
Japanese government IT services is the primary business
NEC.inv: NEC is a diversified Japanese IT company — subsea cable is important but not dominant. Indirect play via TSE listing.

CommScope.name: CommScope
CommScope.about: CommScope's Connectivity & Cable Solutions segment is the fastest-growing division, driven by hyperscale and cloud datacenter GenAI buildout. Q4 2024 net sales +27% YoY with CCS leading all segments. Launched Propel modular fiber platform in 2024 enabling 400G and 800G datacenter applications, reducing installation time by 30%. Strong US manufacturing presence for BABA-compliant broadband infrastructure.
risks: $9B+ debt from 2019 ARRIS acquisition
Not vertically integrated — exposed to strand price fluctuations
Slower to benefit from Ge supply constraint than upstream players
CommScope.inv: COMM is a more leveraged play on US datacenter infrastructure buildout. Higher risk than Corning but also higher upside if datacenter spending continues.

AFL.name: AFL
AFL.about: AFL (Fujikura subsidiary) is a major US cable assembler focused on utility, datacenter, and enterprise fiber optic infrastructure. Produces specialty cables for power utilities, military, and hyperscale datacenter applications. Fully US-based manufacturing makes AFL a BABA-compliant domestic supplier for government-funded broadband and defense programs.
AFL.risk: Private company — no direct investment vehicle.
risks: No listed equity vehicle
Dependent on Fujikura parent strategy
US utility cable market has different demand cycle than datacenter
AFL.inv: No direct investment — privately held Fujikura subsidiary. Indirect exposure via Fujikura (5803 TSE).

Umicore.name: Umicore
Umicore.about: Umicore is a Belgian specialty materials and recycling company listed on Euronext Brussels. It refines platinum, palladium, and rhodium from spent automotive catalysts; recovers cobalt and lithium from used batteries; and produces high-purity germanium, indium, and other materials for semiconductor, fiber optic, and battery customers. The Olen, Belgium facility is the western world's only commercial-scale germanium tetrachloride producer. Operations span Europe, North America, and Asia.
Umicore.risk: Single-facility risk at Olen; business distractions from other Umicore segments.
risks: Single-facility risk at Olen; business distractions from other Umicore segments.
DRC Big Hill ramp timeline uncertain
Recycled scrap feed shrinks if Chinese fiber output declines
Umicore.inv: The western chokepoint for fiber-grade germanium tetrachloride. Operates the only commercial-scale GeCl₄ facility outside China and Russia. The December 2024 Chinese export ban made Umicore's position structural rather than discretionary.

5N Plus.name: 5N Plus
5N Plus.about: 5N Plus is a Canadian specialty metals refiner listed on the Toronto Stock Exchange and headquartered in Montreal. It purifies raw germanium, gallium, indium, tellurium, and bismuth into the high-purity grades that semiconductor wafer producers, satellite solar manufacturers, and defense suppliers require. The company owns AZUR Space, the German subsidiary that produces germanium-based multi-junction solar cells used on satellites — a vertical integration that links 5N Plus's refining capacity directly to a high-margin downstream end use. Operations span Canada, the US, Germany, and Vietnam.
5N Plus.risk: Gallium is a small fraction of 5N Plus revenue; gallium thesis exposure is diluted
risks: Gallium is a small fraction of 5N Plus revenue; gallium thesis exposure is diluted
5N Plus.inv: Most direct western investment vehicle for critical minerals refining broadly. Gallium is a smaller exposure than germanium within 5N Plus's portfolio.

PPM Pure Metals.name: PPM Pure Metals
PPM Pure Metals.about: PPM Pure Metals is a privately held German specialty materials company headquartered in Langelsheim, Lower Saxony. It produces zone-refined germanium, gallium, indium, and other high-purity specialty metals for European semiconductor, IR optics, and SiGe customers. Operating at meaningfully smaller scale than Umicore, PPM serves as the second European refining presence and supplies customers that prefer geographic redundancy or non-Belgian sourcing. The company sits within the broader German specialty chemicals and materials industrial base but is not publicly listed.
PPM Pure Metals.risk: Private — not directly investable. Scale limited.
risks: Private — not directly investable. Scale limited.
Small scale, niche market focus
Dependent on scrap feed from European fiber and IR optics manufacturers
PPM Pure Metals.inv: Privately-held German specialty refiner. Zone-refined germanium for IR optics and SiGe markets; not at fiber-grade GeCl₄ volumes. Part of western refining diversification but smaller scale than Umicore.

Spetsugli.name: Spetsugli
Spetsugli.about: Spetsugli is Russia's primary germanium-bearing coal deposit, located in Primorsky Krai in the Russian Far East. It is a coal-hosted deposit similar in formation type to Lincang and Wulantuga, with reserves estimated at around 1,015 tonnes of contained germanium. Operated by JSC Germanium, the state-owned vertically integrated Russian operator. Following Russia's 2022 invasion of Ukraine and the subsequent western sanctions on JSC Germanium's parent organization, output from Spetsugli is now redirected entirely to non-western buyers, primarily Chinese.
Spetsugli.risk: Sanctions-exposed; no western route to market.
risks: Sanctions-exposed; no western route to market.
JSC Germanium is part of Rostec, a sanctioned state defense conglomerate
Sanctions could be reversed but political timeline is entirely unpredictable
Spetsugli.inv: Russia's primary germanium deposit. Effectively unavailable to western buyers due to sanctions. Output likely flows to Chinese and non-western buyers, reinforcing Chinese supply dominance over western markets.

Big Hill.name: Big Hill
Big Hill.about: Big Hill is a historic copper-cobalt mining tailings dump in Lubumbashi, in the Haut-Katanga province of the DRC. The pile contains the residual waste from decades of colonial-era and post-independence copper-cobalt mining and is being reprocessed by the STL/Gécamines joint venture to recover germanium, cobalt, and copper. Estimated germanium content sits at around 700 tonnes — a meaningful resource, particularly because tailings reprocessing avoids the permitting, exploration, and capital cost overhead of greenfield mining. Umicore holds the germanium offtake rights, locking the Big Hill output to its Olen, Belgium refinery.
Big Hill.risk: DRC operating risk; reprocessing ramp-up delays possible.
risks: DRC operating risk; reprocessing ramp-up delays possible.
Hydrometallurgical plant is new — ramp-up timeline uncertain
Exclusive Umicore offtake means no price competition
Big Hill.inv: The single most important western supply development. ~700 t of germanium-bearing tailings being reprocessed by STL/Gécamines under Umicore offtake. Critical feedstock for Umicore as recycled-only supply saturates.

Red Dog.name: Red Dog
Red Dog.about: Red Dog is a world-class zinc-lead deposit located in northwest Alaska, one of the largest zinc operations globally. The orebody contains germanium as a trace element within the sphalerite zinc mineralization, recovered as a byproduct during smelting at Teck's Trail facility in British Columbia. Operated by Teck Resources, Red Dog is currently scheduled to deplete by 2031 under existing mine plans, with the adjacent Aktigiruq extension prospect under evaluation as a potential successor. If Aktigiruq does not advance, the only North American commercial source of germanium-bearing zinc concentrate disappears with Red Dog's closure.
Red Dog.risk: Declining toward planned end-of-mine in 2031 (Aktigiruq extension possible but not yet permitted).
risks: Declining toward planned end-of-mine in 2031 (Aktigiruq extension possible but not yet permitted).
Germanium output volumes not publicly disclosed
Teck-Anglo American merger may change operational priorities
Red Dog.inv: North America's only significant western germanium-bearing zinc operation. Teck Resources operates and refines through Trail smelter; exposure to US Defense Production Act Title III investments possible.

JSC Germanium.name: JSC Germanium
JSC Germanium.about: JSC Germanium is a Russian state-owned vertically integrated germanium producer headquartered in Krasnoyarsk. It mines the Spetsugli germanium-bearing coal deposit in Primorsky Krai in the Russian Far East, refines the recovered material in-house, and produces a complete range of germanium products including refined metal, dioxide, fiber-grade tetrachloride, IR optics blanks, and wafers. Historically a meaningful contributor to global germanium supply with around 20 t/yr of refined output, JSC Germanium is now sanctioned from western markets following Russia's 2022 invasion of Ukraine, with output redirected to Chinese and other non-western buyers.
JSC Germanium.risk: Sanctions-exposed; no western route to market.
risks: Sanctions-exposed; no western route to market.
Part of Rostec defense conglomerate
Production continues but serves only Russia and potentially China
JSC Germanium.inv: Russia's integrated germanium operator. Effectively unavailable to western buyers due to sanctions. Tracked for geopolitical signaling but not investable.

STL / Gécamines.name: STL / Gécamines
STL / Gécamines.about: STL/Gécamines is a private joint venture between La Société de Traitement du Terril de Lubumbashi and Gécamines, the Congolese state-owned mining company. It operates in Lubumbashi, in the Haut-Katanga province of the DRC, where it reprocesses the Big Hill tailings dump — the residual waste from decades of historical Belgian-era copper and cobalt mining. Big Hill contains an estimated 700+ tonnes of recoverable germanium alongside its copper and cobalt content, making it one of the largest single germanium resources outside China.
STL / Gécamines.risk: DRC operating risk; JV structure and political sensitivity around Gécamines.
risks: DRC operating risk; JV structure and political sensitivity around Gécamines.
Hydrometallurgical plant is new — ramp-up timeline uncertain
Exclusive Umicore offtake means no market price competition
STL / Gécamines.inv: Most important western supply development in the chain. Feeds directly into Umicore under offtake — the tailings flow is what enables Umicore's continued non-Chinese output at scale.

Teck Resources.name: Teck Resources
Teck Resources.about: Teck Resources is a Canadian diversified mining company listed on the NYSE and TSX, headquartered in Vancouver. It mines and processes copper, zinc, and lead from large-scale operations in Canada, Chile, Peru, and the United States, with copper as the largest revenue contributor following the divestiture of its steelmaking coal business in 2024. Teck operates the Red Dog zinc mine in Alaska — one of the largest zinc operations globally — and the Trail smelter in British Columbia, where germanium is recovered as a byproduct of zinc concentrate processing. In September 2025, Teck announced a merger of equals with Anglo American to form Anglo Teck, with shareholder and Investment Canada Act approvals secured by December 2025; the deal awaits remaining regulatory approvals through 2026.
Teck Resources.risk: Red Dog declines toward 2031 end-of-mine; Ge output volumes not separately disclosed.
risks: Red Dog declines toward 2031 end-of-mine; Ge output volumes not separately disclosed.
Teck-Anglo American merger may change operational priorities at Red Dog
Germanium revenue is unquantified
Teck Resources.inv: North America's only significant western germanium operator via Red Dog. Germanium is a small line in Teck's overall business but carries strategic weight for US supply chain diversification.

Dycom Industries.name: Dycom Industries
Dycom Industries.about: Dycom is the largest dedicated fiber and telecommunications contractor in the United States, installing underground and aerial fiber networks for AT&T, Verizon, Lumen, and increasingly hyperscalers building private fiber WANs. Revenues surpassed $4.7B in FY2025, driven by BEAD-funded rural broadband and hyperscaler private network programs.
Dycom Industries.risk: Customer concentration — AT&T and Lumen represent majority of revenue.
risks: Customer concentration — AT&T and Lumen represent majority of revenue
Labor-intensive model with thin margins subject to cost inflation
Hyperscaler in-house build teams reduce addressable market over time
Dycom Industries.inv: DY (NYSE) is the most direct public vehicle for fiber installation exposure. As hyperscalers shift from leasing to building private fiber networks — Microsoft's 120,000-mile private WAN being the leading example — Dycom captures the labor-intensive installation phase that no hyperscaler performs in-house.

MasTec.name: MasTec
MasTec.about: MasTec is one of the largest specialty contractors in North America, with infrastructure segments spanning communications (fiber, 5G), power delivery, oil/gas pipelines, and renewables. Its communications segment installs fiber networks and 5G infrastructure for AT&T, DirecTV, and regional telcos, as well as datacenter-related cabling for enterprise customers.
MasTec.risk: Diversified into oil/gas and renewables — fiber is a minority segment.
risks: Diversified model dilutes pure fiber exposure
Margins under pressure from project mix and labor costs
Integration risk from recent acquisitions
MasTec.inv: MTZ (NYSE) offers diversified infrastructure exposure with fiber as a significant but minority segment. The communications division benefits from the same BEAD and hyperscaler-driven fiber deployment cycle as Dycom, but MasTec's power delivery segment also benefits from datacenter power infrastructure buildout — double exposure to the AI buildout.

MYR Group.name: MYR Group
MYR Group.about: MYR Group provides electrical construction services through two segments: Transmission and Distribution (T&D) and Commercial and Industrial (C&I). Its C&I segment installs electrical systems in large commercial buildings including datacenters — wiring, power distribution, emergency systems, and increasingly the high-density power infrastructure required for GPU clusters.
MYR Group.risk: Commercial & industrial segment is the core — fiber is indirect exposure.
risks: Fiber is indirect exposure — primary business is electrical contracting
Labor cost inflation pressures margins
C&I segment competes with many regional electrical contractors
MYR Group.inv: MYRG (NASDAQ) benefits from the datacenter electrical infrastructure buildout — every hyperscale facility requires millions of dollars of electrical work. As datacenter power density increases from 10kW to 100kW+ per rack for AI, electrical installation complexity and contract value per facility increases proportionally.

Wesco International.name: Wesco International
Wesco International.about: Wesco International is the largest US distributor of electrical and electronic products and supply chain services, including a significant communications and fiber segment. It sources fiber optic cable, connectors, conduit, and related equipment from Corning, Prysmian, and other manufacturers and distributes to electrical contractors, systems integrators, and datacenter builders. Acquired Anixter in 2020 to build out the communications/fiber distribution segment.
Wesco International.risk: Distributor margin compression as hyperscalers develop direct manufacturer relationships.
risks: Distributor margin compression as hyperscalers develop direct manufacturer relationships
Fiber price spike benefits revenues but inventory risk increases
Commoditization pressure on distribution margins long-term
Wesco International.inv: WCC (NYSE) benefits from the fiber supply chain buildup — as fiber prices surge and availability tightens, distributors with supplier relationships and inventory positions gain pricing power. Wesco's communications segment captures a percentage of every fiber deployment without the installation labor execution risk.

Turner Construction.name: Turner Construction
Turner Construction.about: Turner Construction is the largest general contractor by revenue in the United States and the dominant datacenter construction firm. Approximately 37% of its entire project backlog is datacenter construction — a concentration unmatched by any peer. Turner builds hyperscale facilities for Microsoft, Meta, Google, and AWS across North America and Europe, managing the full construction lifecycle from foundation to commissioning.
Turner Construction.risk: Private company — no direct investment. DC concentration creates cyclicality exposure.
risks: Private company — no direct investment vehicle
37% datacenter concentration creates significant cyclicality exposure
Labor and material cost inflation pressures fixed-price construction contracts
Turner Construction.inv: No direct public investment — Turner is privately held. The investment angle is indirect: Turner's order backlog is the clearest leading indicator of datacenter construction activity. Turner's dominance means hyperscaler capex plans translate directly into Turner revenues — but the play is the customers, not the contractor.

Mortenson.name: Mortenson
Mortenson.about: Mortenson is one of the top five datacenter construction firms in the United States, with a strong presence in modular and prefabricated datacenter construction for hyperscalers. It has built facilities for Meta, Microsoft, and Google, and has developed proprietary prefabrication techniques that reduce construction timelines for large-scale deployments. Employee-owned and privately held.
Mortenson.risk: Private company. DC market share below Turner and Holder — subject to pricing competition.
risks: Private — no investment vehicle
Modular approach reduces labor requirements but requires factory capacity
Competition from Turner, DPR, and Holder for hyperscaler relationships
Mortenson.inv: No direct investment — private company. Mortenson's modular datacenter expertise is increasingly relevant as hyperscalers seek to accelerate build timelines. The investment angle is hyperscaler capex execution risk — Mortenson is one of a small number of contractors capable of delivering 100MW+ facilities on schedule.

DPR Construction.name: DPR Construction
DPR Construction.about: DPR Construction is a top-ten US general contractor with a strong focus on technology sector and mission-critical facilities including datacenters. Its Silicon Valley roots give it deep relationships with technology companies. DPR specializes in complex, high-specification builds requiring tight coordination between electrical, mechanical, and IT systems — the profile demanded by AI-ready hyperscale facilities.
DPR Construction.risk: Private company. Smaller scale than Turner — limited to specific hyperscaler programs.
risks: Private — no investment vehicle
Smaller scale limits ability to compete for the largest hyperscaler projects
Technology sector concentration creates correlation with tech capex cycles
DPR Construction.inv: No direct investment — private company. DPR's technology sector expertise positions it well for the AI datacenter buildout, but the scale is smaller than Turner or Hyperscaler Self-Build programs.

Holder Construction.name: Holder Construction
Holder Construction.about: Holder Construction is one of the leading datacenter construction companies in the southeastern and mid-Atlantic United States, with significant projects for Google (multiple Virginia facilities) and Microsoft. The company is private with revenue not publicly disclosed. Holder's strength is in Northern Virginia, which hosts the world's highest concentration of hyperscale datacenters by megawatt capacity.
Holder Construction.risk: Private company. Revenue undisclosed. Southeast and mid-Atlantic focus limits geographic reach.
risks: Private — no investment vehicle
Revenue and backlog undisclosed — no visibility into pipeline
Northern Virginia concentration creates geographic single-point exposure
Holder Construction.inv: No direct investment. Holder's Northern Virginia concentration is strategically valuable given that region's dominance in US datacenter capacity, but the company is not investable.

Digital Realty.name: Digital Realty
Digital Realty.about: Digital Realty is the world's largest datacenter REIT by market cap, operating colocation and wholesale datacenter facilities globally. Its portfolio is heavily concentrated in Northern Virginia, which hosts the world's densest hyperscale datacenter cluster. Digital Realty leases space and power to hyperscalers, enterprises, and interconnection-dependent businesses, taking approximately 15% of US leased datacenter power.
Digital Realty.risk: Northern Virginia power moratorium creates near-term capacity constraint.
risks: Northern Virginia power moratorium creates near-term capacity constraints
REIT structure requires high dividend payout, limiting retained capital for development
Hyperscaler self-build trend reduces long-term colocation demand
Digital Realty.inv: DLR (NYSE) offers REIT-structured exposure to datacenter infrastructure with dividend yield characteristics. Northern Virginia concentration is a double-edged sword — the market is the largest but faces power moratoriums and land scarcity. Digital Realty's wholesale model means hyperscaler spending flows through it rather than around it.

G42.name: G42
G42.about: G42 is the UAE government's primary artificial intelligence and technology investment vehicle, backed by the Abu Dhabi royal family. In 2024, Microsoft invested $1.5 billion in G42 in exchange for G42 adopting Azure as its primary cloud platform and committing to US technology standards. G42 is building hyperscale AI datacenters in Abu Dhabi to position the UAE as a sovereign AI infrastructure hub independent of Chinese technology.
G42.risk: Geopolitical exposure — historical Huawei ties created US government scrutiny.
risks: Historical Huawei technology partnerships created US national security scrutiny
Sovereign ownership means strategic priorities can override commercial logic
UAE geopolitical position between US and China technology blocs creates binary risk
G42.inv: No direct public investment — G42 is private. The investment angle is Microsoft (MSFT), which is G42's technology partner and cloud provider. G42's datacenter buildout is a revenue opportunity for Azure and increases Microsoft's footprint in the Middle East AI market.

NEOM / PIF.name: NEOM / PIF
NEOM / PIF.about: NEOM is Saudi Arabia's flagship Vision 2030 megaproject, backed by the Public Investment Fund with a stated budget of $500 billion. The project includes THE LINE (a 170km linear city), OXAGON (an industrial complex), and SINDALAH (a tourism island). NEOM's digital infrastructure requirements — including AI datacenters for a fully automated smart city — represent a significant latent demand source for fiber optic cable and associated supply chains, though actual deployment remains years away.
NEOM / PIF.risk: Megaproject execution risk at unprecedented scale. $500B commitment vs. $6.4B spent through 2023.
risks: Megaproject execution risk — $500B committed vs. $6.4B actually spent through 2023
Project scope reductions already announced — THE LINE scaled back significantly
Political risk — government priorities can change, especially with oil price volatility
NEOM / PIF.inv: No direct investment — NEOM is a sovereign project. The Saudi PIF is a sovereign wealth fund with no public vehicle. The investment angle is as a signal of Gulf state demand for AI infrastructure that will eventually require fiber, germanium, and associated supply chains from western providers.

Various State Operators.name: Various State Operators
Various State Operators.about: Various State Operators is an aggregate node representing the smaller Chinese state-affiliated coal operators that work germanium-bearing lignite deposits across the Yimin basin and other secondary regions in Inner Mongolia and northern China. Individual operators are typically county-level or municipal-level state enterprises with limited public disclosure, opaque ownership structures, and small individual germanium output volumes. Combined, this category represents an estimated 5-10 tonnes per year of crude germanium extraction — a meaningful supplemental fraction of Chinese supply but smaller than the named majors.
risks: Fragmented, opaque operator base.
Opaque operations with no public reporting
Output highly variable
Various State Operators.inv: Aggregate of smaller Chinese state-affiliated coal operators. Represents fragmented supply with limited responsiveness to price signals.

Chinese State Refiners.name: Chinese State Refiners
Chinese State Refiners.about: Smaller Chinese Refiners is an aggregate node representing the long tail of Chinese germanium refining capacity beyond the named majors — Yunnan Chihong Refinery and Lincang Xinyuan Refinery. Individual operators are typically smaller specialty chemicals companies or regional state enterprises that focus on germanium dioxide and crude metal output rather than fiber-grade GeCl₄, where the technical bar is higher. Combined output is estimated around 14 t/yr. Few are publicly listed; most operate within Chinese supply chains with limited disclosure to international markets.
Chinese State Refiners.risk: Opaque individual facility data; output subject to Chinese export controls.
risks: Opaque individual facility data; output subject to Chinese export controls.
High exit risk when prices fall
Opaque output reporting
Chinese State Refiners.inv: Aggregate of smaller Chinese refiners. Focus on GeO₂ and crude metal; limited fiber-grade GeCl₄ capacity at individual facilities.

Blue Moon Metals.name: Blue Moon Metals
Blue Moon Metals.about: Blue Moon Metals is a Canadian junior mining company developing five brownfield polymetallic projects across the United States and Norway, listed on the TSX Venture Exchange and NASDAQ. The Apex mine in Washington County, southern Utah, is one of those projects and the only past-producing primary germanium and gallium mine in the United States. Blue Moon acquired Apex from Teck American (a Teck Resources subsidiary) and is restarting development with backing from Oaktree Capital, Hartree Partners, Wheaton Precious Metals, and Altius Minerals. Teck retained an 8% equity stake in Blue Moon as part of the acquisition.
Blue Moon Metals.risk: Pre-production junior; permitting, financing, and ramp-up risk typical of early-stage mining projects.
risks: Pre-production junior; permitting, financing, and ramp-up risk typical of early-stage mining projects.
No modern NI 43-101 resource estimate yet
2028 production target is aspirational
Blue Moon Metals.inv: Most important US-domiciled germanium supply development. First new primary Ge mine on US soil in decades. Success would materially reduce US dependency on Chinese primary supply.

LightPath Technologies.name: LightPath Technologies
LightPath Technologies.about: LightPath Technologies is a US-listed infrared optics and imaging systems company headquartered in Orlando, Florida. It designs and manufactures infrared lenses, thermal imaging cameras, and complete optical assemblies for defense, surveillance, and industrial inspection customers. LightPath holds an exclusive license to BlackDiamond chalcogenide glass, an alternative material to germanium for IR optics applications. The company is in the middle of a strategic transition from selling individual lens components to selling complete imaging systems, which carries higher margins. Listed on NASDAQ with manufacturing operations in Florida, New Hampshire, Latvia, and China.
LightPath Technologies.risk: Substitution penetration is slow; BlackDiamond requires design-in cycles that take years per customer platform.
risks: Substitution penetration is slow; BlackDiamond requires design-in cycles that take years per customer platform.
Pre-profit company competing against Umicore's GASIR®
Germanium price decline would reduce urgency for substitution
LightPath Technologies.inv: The substitution hedge on germanium scarcity. If germanium prices remain elevated or supply tightens further, BlackDiamond glass captures displaced IR optics demand. Downstream of the germanium chain but structurally important to the investment thesis.

Guinea Bauxite.name: Guinea Bauxite
Guinea Bauxite.about: Guinea hosts the world's largest bauxite reserves (~7.4 billion tonnes) and is the largest bauxite exporter globally. CBG (51% Halco / 49% Guinea government) operates Sangaredi. SMB-Winning (Chinese-led consortium) operates Boke. Most Guinean bauxite is exported unrefined — primarily to China — because Guinea has minimal domestic alumina capacity.
Guinea Bauxite.risk: Political stability in Guinea; Chinese strategic lock-in via SMB-Winning consortium ownership
risks: Political stability in Guinea; Chinese strategic lock-in via SMB-Winning consortium ownership
Guinea Bauxite.inv: Guinea is the world's largest bauxite exporter but refines almost none of its own output. Roughly 80% of Guinean bauxite flows to Chinese refineries, meaning Guinea structurally underwrites Chinese gallium dominance.

Australian Bauxite.name: Australian Bauxite
Australian Bauxite.about: Australia is the world's largest bauxite producer at ~100M t/yr. Unlike Guinea, most Australian bauxite is refined domestically through integrated operations. Rio Tinto's Weipa feeds Yarwun and Queensland Alumina. Alcoa's Huntly and Willowdale feed Kwinana, Pinjarra, and Wagerup — the last being the planned JAGA gallium project site.
Australian Bauxite.risk: Project-level execution risk at JAGA; FID delays
risks: Project-level execution risk at JAGA; FID delays
Australian Bauxite.inv: Australia is the only major bauxite-producing region with domestic alumina refining at scale AND a western-aligned political posture. The Wagerup refinery operated by Alcoa — the planned JAGA gallium site — processes Australian-origin bauxite.

Chinese Domestic Bauxite.name: Chinese Domestic Bauxite
Chinese Domestic Bauxite.about: China produces ~90M t/yr of bauxite domestically and imports an additional ~130M t/yr, primarily from Guinea. The combined feedstock pool feeds ~20 Chinese alumina refineries, most of which have installed gallium recovery circuits. This is where ~83% of global primary gallium is produced.
Chinese Domestic Bauxite.risk: None chain-specific; Chinese integrated control is a feature, not a risk, from Chinese policy perspective
risks: None chain-specific; Chinese integrated control is a feature from Chinese policy perspective
Chinese Domestic Bauxite.inv: China's combined domestic bauxite production and imports feed the ~20 Chinese alumina refineries that produce ~83% of global primary gallium.

Brazilian Bauxite.name: Brazilian Bauxite
Brazilian Bauxite.about: Brazil is the world's fourth-largest bauxite producer. MRN Trombetas (Vale, BHP, Rio Tinto, Alcoa, Norsk Hydro, CBA consortium) produces ~12M t/yr. Alcoa's Juruti adds ~6M t/yr. Combined output primarily feeds Alunorte (Norsk Hydro) — the world's largest alumina refinery — which does not currently produce gallium.
Brazilian Bauxite.risk: No active project; latent supply may never materialize without specific catalyst
risks: No active project; latent supply may never materialize without specific catalyst
Brazilian Bauxite.inv: Brazil's bauxite feeds Alunorte, the world's largest alumina refinery, which currently does not produce gallium. A latent supply source — if any operator decides to retrofit recovery circuits.

Indonesian Bauxite.name: Indonesian Bauxite
Indonesian Bauxite.about: Indonesia banned unprocessed bauxite exports in June 2023, pushing domestic refining investment. Most output currently still reaches Chinese refineries via various workarounds. Strategic relevance is optionality — large reserves could support future gallium-producing refineries if domestic alumina capacity scales.
Indonesian Bauxite.risk: Export ban enforcement variability; no direct gallium investment vehicle
risks: Export ban enforcement variability; no direct gallium investment vehicle
Indonesian Bauxite.inv: Indonesia's 2023 unrefined-export ban is reshaping bauxite flows globally. Limited current relevance to gallium production — strategic optionality if domestic Indonesian alumina capacity scales.

Chinese Bauxite Refineries.name: Chinese Bauxite Refineries
Chinese Bauxite Refineries.location_detail: Shandong, Guangxi, Henan, Shanxi, Yunnan, China
Chinese Bauxite Refineries.about: ~20 Chinese alumina refineries produce gallium as a byproduct of the Bayer process. Operators include state-owned Chalco and Shandong Weiqiao plus private Vital Materials and Zhuzhou Keneng. Chinese capacity grew from ~140 t/yr in 2010 to ~1,000 t/yr by 2022.
Chinese Bauxite Refineries.risk: Export licensing policy cycles; utilization decisions at Beijing's discretion
risks: Export licensing policy cycles; utilization decisions at Beijing's discretion
Chinese Bauxite Refineries.inv: The structural core of global gallium supply. ~590 t/yr actual production across ~20 refineries at ~59% utilization represents China's ability to scale output up or down by hundreds of tonnes per year based on policy direction.

Alcoa / JAGA (Wagerup).name: Alcoa / JAGA (Wagerup)
Alcoa / JAGA (Wagerup).about: Japan-Australia Gallium (JAGA) project announced October 2025 to extract gallium from Alcoa's existing Wagerup alumina refinery liquor stream. Backed by four sovereign governments (US, Australia, Japan) with AUD 200M concessional Australian equity.
Alcoa / JAGA (Wagerup).risk: FID delay; sovereign backing concentration creates political dependency
risks: FID delay; sovereign backing concentration creates political dependency
Alcoa / JAGA (Wagerup).inv: The single largest non-Chinese primary production facility in development. 100 t/yr at full ramp would be roughly 30% of current global refined supply.

Alcoa Corporation.name: Alcoa Corporation
Alcoa Corporation.location_detail: Pittsburgh PA (HQ); Australia, North America, Brazil
Alcoa Corporation.about: Alcoa is one of the world's largest integrated aluminum producers, with bauxite mining, alumina refining, and aluminum smelting operations across Australia, North America, Brazil, and Europe. Gallium is a new product line that would leverage existing Wagerup refinery infrastructure.
Alcoa Corporation.risk: Gallium thesis is small relative to aluminum; aluminum price volatility drives share price more than JAGA milestones
risks: Gallium thesis is small relative to aluminum; aluminum price volatility drives share price
Alcoa Corporation.inv: The corporate vehicle for gallium exposure via the JAGA project. Given sovereign backing, Alcoa is the most liquid western vehicle for primary production exposure.

Metlen Energy & Metals.name: Metlen Energy & Metals
Metlen Energy & Metals.about: Diversified Greek industrial group with metals, energy, and infrastructure operations. Operates one of Europe's largest alumina refineries at Agios Nikolaos. Announced first 5 kg gallium production January 2026. Designated EU Critical Raw Materials Act Strategic Project.
Metlen Energy & Metals.risk: Scale-up execution; 10,000x ramp from 5 kg to 50 tonnes
risks: Scale-up execution; Greek sovereign risk; European demand capture vs export
Metlen Energy & Metals.inv: Europe's first industrial gallium producer. 50 t/yr by 2028 would fully cover current EU gallium demand. Already in production at demo scale.

Rio Tinto / Indium JV.name: Rio Tinto / Indium JV
Rio Tinto / Indium JV.about: World's second-largest mining company with significant bauxite and alumina operations. Vaudreuil gallium project is a partnership with Indium Corporation (which owns the extraction process IP). Demo plant began operating 2024.
Rio Tinto / Indium JV.risk: Commercial-scale FID not yet made; gallium thesis diluted by mega-cap scale
risks: Commercial-scale FID not yet made; gallium thesis diluted by mega-cap scale
Rio Tinto / Indium JV.inv: Demonstration-stage North American producer with 40 t/yr commercial target post-2028. Less binary than Alcoa (demo already running) but further from commercial scale.

Korea Zinc / Crucible JV.name: Korea Zinc / Crucible JV
Korea Zinc / Crucible JV.about: $7.4B joint venture announced December 2025. Korea Zinc (<10% equity, operator role), US DoD (40% equity), JPMorgan (advisor). Largest critical minerals processing investment in US history.
Korea Zinc / Crucible JV.risk: Construction timing (2026-2029); ownership dispute affects execution
risks: Construction timing (2026-2029); ownership dispute (Korea Zinc vs Young Poong) affects execution
Korea Zinc / Crucible JV.inv: 40 t/yr gallium from a multi-output smelter scheduled 2029. The US DoD 40% equity stake signals strategic-asset treatment of gallium alongside other outputs.

Nyrstar Tennessee.name: Nyrstar Tennessee
Nyrstar Tennessee.about: Trafigura-owned zinc smelter operating since the 1970s. Zinc concentrate feed contains significant gallium but recovery was never commercially implemented. Being demolished to make way for Korea Zinc / Crucible JV.
Nyrstar Tennessee.risk: Demolition execution timing affects Crucible construction schedule
risks: Demolition execution timing affects Crucible construction schedule
Nyrstar Tennessee.inv: Historic zinc smelter illustrating the 'lost' western recovery opportunity that the Crucible rebuild is meant to finally capture.

Dowa Holdings.name: Dowa Holdings
Dowa Holdings.about: Diversified Japanese specialty materials group. Japan's primary high-purity refining capacity at 6N+ — the grade compound semiconductor wafer production requires. Dependent on imported feedstock (Chinese primary plus Japanese recycled).
Dowa Holdings.risk: Feedstock dependence on Chinese primary supply; diversified portfolio dilutes gallium thesis
risks: Feedstock dependence on Chinese primary supply; diversified portfolio dilutes gallium thesis
Dowa Holdings.inv: The de facto western pipeline for GaAs wafer supply. Japan's primary high-purity gallium refiner at the 6N+ purity grade required for compound semiconductor wafer production.

Indium Corporation.name: Indium Corporation
Indium Corporation.about: Privately-held specialty materials company known for solder, indium, and specialty metals refining. Owns the gallium extraction process IP used in Rio Tinto's Vaudreuil demo plant. Operates GaAs manufacturing scrap recycling in New York.
Indium Corporation.risk: Private; limited visibility; IP value crystallizes only if Rio Tinto commercial conversion goes ahead
risks: Private; limited visibility; IP value crystallizes only if Rio Tinto commercial conversion goes ahead
Indium Corporation.inv: Owns the process IP used in Rio Tinto's Vaudreuil demo plant. More of an IP-licensing and recycling play than a volume producer. Private, so no direct investment vehicle.

Vital Materials.name: Vital Materials
Vital Materials.about: One of China's largest integrated specialty metals producers. Refines Ga, In, Ge, Te, rare earths. Produces MOCVD precursor chemicals (trimethylgallium) used to grow thin GaN layers during chip manufacturing.
Vital Materials.risk: Private Chinese company; subject to same export licensing regime
risks: Private Chinese company; subject to same export licensing regime
Vital Materials.inv: Core Chinese high-purity gallium refiner. Produces MOCVD precursor chemicals — the specific chemical form gallium takes in GaN chip manufacturing.

Zhuzhou Smelter Group.name: Zhuzhou Smelter Group
Zhuzhou Smelter Group.about: Two Hunan-based Chinese operators of high-purity gallium refining capacity. Zhuzhou Smelter Group is state-affiliated; Zhuzhou Keneng is private. Together with Vital Materials, comprise the core of Chinese downstream high-purity gallium production.
Zhuzhou Smelter Group.risk: Subject to Chinese export licensing regime
risks: Subject to Chinese export licensing regime
Zhuzhou Smelter Group.inv: Together with Vital Materials, comprise core Chinese downstream high-purity gallium production serving the Chinese semiconductor industry.
